what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the multi-scale embedding pyramid (Group 11). It creates deterministic synthetic blob vectors, runs agglomerative clustering through runtime.scale, and asserts nesting of dendrogram cuts, Ward linkage behavior, centroid properties, coarse-versus-fine resolution over a temporary FsStore, persisted parent links, incremental rebuild, and dyadic rung derivation.
observations: Hermetic: uses a temporary FsStore and deterministic synthetic vectors, no live server or real store.
Divided into six numbered sections, each locking a specific invariant (nesting, Ward vs average, centroid, coarse≠fine, persisted nesting/incremental, fail-loud/dyadic rungs).
Synthetic vectors are built from near-orthogonal one-hot directions with deterministic jitter, so blob membership is ground truth.
Uses private helpers scale._norm and scale._cos directly in tests.
Global mutable PASS/FAIL counters are incremented by a local check helper.
Verifies incremental rebuild by inspecting built and skipped counts returned by build_scale_pyramid.
Checks that default_rungs(162) == [32, 8] and that rungs are powers of two descending.
Asserts that coarse-rung results are cluster:// addresses and that the top coarse theme contains the fine top-1 unit.
standouts: The NESTING LOCK: proves that cuts from one agglomerative dendrogram nest, so fine clusters are subsets of coarse clusters.
The WARD LOCK: demonstrates average-linkage chaining on a dense-core-plus-satellites synthetic and shows Ward stays balanced.
The COARSE≠FINE LOCK: a query at the theme rung returns a cluster address that contains the fine-rung top unit, not the same object.
Incremental, content-hash-keyed rebuild is verified via built/skipped counts.
conventions: Heavy docstring with a WHY section explaining design rationale and locks before code.
Section markers with Unicode line-drawing characters and section numbers.
Local check helper with global counters for pass/fail bookkeeping.
sys.path insertion to locate the company package.
try/finally cleanup of the temporary directory.
Fail-loud assertions: expects ValueError for empty spaces, missing rungs, unknown linkage, and dimension mismatch.
concerns: Tests depend on private implementation details such as scale._norm, scale._cos, scale.build_scale_pyramid.__kwdefaults__, and the children_finer field in persisted rungs.
Global mutable PASS/FAIL state makes the suite non-reentrant and harder to run in parallel.
Hard-coded expected default_rungs values (e.g., 162 -> [32, 8]) tie tests to a specific rung-derivation formula.
The check helper prints to stdout and mutates globals, mixing reporting with assertion logic.
Imports FsStore directly and seeds vectors using store-specific address conventions, coupling the test to the filesystem store implementation.
notes: A deterministic, hermetic acceptance test that treats the scale pyramid's nesting, Ward linkage, and coarse/fine resolution as architectural locks.
questions: What is the exact contract of store.index_addresses and store.space_address?
What schema does scale.load_pyramid return for rungs and clusters?
How does build_scale_pyramid compute built versus skipped counts?
What is the live topics-space pyramid verification by use mentioned in the docstring?
What other Group 11 components exist outside this test file?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it guards the scale axis invariants described in the docstring through concrete assertions.
apparent_themes: Hermetic acceptance testing with deterministic synthetic data.
Hierarchical clustering nesting as a correctness invariant.
Ward linkage to avoid chaining and preserve balanced clusters.
Multi-resolution retrieval where coarse rungs are themes containing fine units.
Fail-loud validation of inputs and dimensions.
intention_signals: The file is trying to make the multi-scale pyramid's architectural choices irreversible by encoding them as acceptance-test locks, using synthetic examples to prove why Ward and single-dendrogram nesting are required rather than optional.
novelty: It frames acceptance tests as explicit locks (NESTING, WARD, COARSE≠FINE) and includes a synthetic demonstration of average-linkage chaining to justify the Ward default, which is unusual for a test file.
contribution: A self-contained, deterministic acceptance test that documents and enforces the expected invariants of the scale pyramid—nesting, Ward linkage, centroid semantics, coarse/fine resolution, persisted parent links, incremental rebuild, and dyadic rung derivation—using synthetic vectors and a temporary store.
summary_for_embedding: projection_scale_acceptance.py is a deterministic hermetic acceptance test suite for the multi-scale embedding pyramid that validates agglomerative nesting, Ward linkage, centroid computation, coarse-versus-fine resolution, persisted cross-rung parent links, incremental rebuild, and dyadic rung derivation using synthetic blob vectors and a temporary FsStore.
intention_for_embedding: To lock the Group 11 scale-axis invariants so that regressions to non-nested clustering, average-linkage chaining, or coarse/fine collapse are caught before the live topics-space pyramid is affected.

symbols.does: Increments global PASS/FAIL counters and prints a pass/fail line for a named assertion.
symbols.description: It is the local assertion helper used throughout the acceptance suite; it mutates global PASS/FAIL counters and prints a checkmark or cross with an optional detail string, centralizing the suite's pass/fail bookkeeping and output formatting.

symbols.does: Returns deterministic synthetic vectors and blob labels for well-separated clusters.
symbols.description: It builds reproducible, non-random D-dimensional vectors grouped into B blobs of P points each, using a dominant one-hot axis plus tiny deterministic jitter, so that acceptance tests have a ground-truth coarsening without relying on randomness or external data.
